chief_complaint: A 76-year-old man suddenly lost consciousness at home.
history: The patient was assessed to be in cardiac and respiratory arrest. He had a history of cardiogenic shock, iatrogenic Type B26 aortic dissection, dilated cardiomyopathy, coronary heart disease, and atrial fibrillation.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG: Cardiac arrest. Bloods: Not provided. Imaging: Computed Tomography Angiography (CTA) and Transesophageal Echocardiography (TEE) were performed.
